operating_state: Revenue increased 23% to $6.7 billion for Q2 2021 and 19% to $13.5 billion for the first half, driven by volume growth across key products including Trulicity, Taltz, and Verzenio, with favorable foreign exchange rates contributing 3 percentage points. COVID-19 antibodies generated revenue through Emergency Use Authorizations. Basaglar faced significant competitive pressure with declining U.S. revenue due to lower realized prices and decreased demand.
cost_structure: Gross margin declined to 71.0% in Q2 from 77.8% prior year, primarily due to $423 million inventory impairment charges for excess COVID-19 antibody inventory resulting from reduced government demand and near-term expiry dates. Operating expenses increased 18% in Q2 driven by higher late-stage development costs, COVID-19 therapy research expenses, and increased marketing spend as pandemic-related reductions normalized.
material_events: Acquired Prevail Therapeutics for $747 million in January 2021, gaining gene therapy programs for neurodegenerative diseases including GBA1 gene therapy for Parkinson's disease. FDA revoked Emergency Use Authorization for bamlanivimab alone in April 2021, and U.S. government halted shipments of bamlanivimab/etesevimab combination in June due to variant resistance. Received DOJ subpoena in May 2021 requesting documents relating to Branchburg manufacturing site.
macro_exposures: Alimta vitamin regimen patents expired in major European countries and Japan in June 2021, with U.S. patent protection extending through May 2022 but limited generic entry beginning February 2022. Cymbalta compound patent expired in Japan with generic competitors entering in June 2021. Continued exposure to foreign currency fluctuations primarily against euro and Japanese yen.
forward_outlook: Updated 2021 earnings per share guidance to $6.73-$6.93 range and revenue guidance to $26.8-$27.4 billion, reflecting increased core business revenue offset by reduced COVID-19 therapy revenue expectations. Management intends to submit tirzepatide for Type 2 diabetes regulatory approval by end of 2021 following successful Phase III trials, and donanemab for early Alzheimer's disease under FDA accelerated approval pathway by year-end after receiving Breakthrough Therapy designation.
uncertainty_profile: DOJ investigation ongoing regarding Branchburg manufacturing site with Lilly conducting independent investigation through external counsel. Faces ongoing COVID-19 antibody inventory risks from variant mutations reducing effectiveness and changing government demand patterns, with potential for additional impairments. Continued competitive pressures on Basaglar expected to result in price declines and market share loss.
